MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. DEFENDANT NRG ENERGY CENTER SAN FRANCISCO, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR. MOTION WAS ADVANCED TO 2/6/07 ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. (301/PJB) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189

Defendant Nrg Enrgy Center San Francisco Llc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-06-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT NRG ENERGY CENTER SAN FRANCISCO LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO PROVIDE THE MANDATORY 75 DAYS STATUTORY NOTICE. THE EARLIEST DATE TO NOTICE THE HEARING WOULD HAVE BEEN 2/13/07. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (301/PJB/JF) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-06-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT MODIFIED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING BY CORRECTING THE PLAINTIFF'S NAME. THE COURT'S RULING IS AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED, THE OBJECTION TO PARAGRAPH 5 OF THE MURPHY DECLARATION IS SUSTAINED. THE CITY HAS FAILED TO SHIFT IT'S BURDEN BECAUSE IT HAS NOT PRESENTED ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT PACIFIC BELL CANNOT PROVE THAT CITY STEAM CAUSED PACIFIC BELL'S DAMAGE. IN ANY EVENT THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO THE SOURCE OF THE STEAM THAT CAUSED THE DAMAGE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (301/PJB/JF) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189

Re-Notice Of Hearing On Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Jan-26-2007 Text Ruling

John P. Aubrey of the Office of the City Attorney, 554-3968, appearing for defendant and moving party
Gerard J. Donnellan of AT & T Services, Inc. Legal Department, 778-1358, appearing for plaintiff and responding party

The Court made a personal disclosure, and no objections were raised. The matter was not argued and DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings was granted with 10 days leave to amend. Order signed in open Court. (301/PJB/JF) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189
